title: ZAP Lesson 1: Overview and Modes
summary: Learn how ZAP works as an intercepting proxy and how modes control risk.
content:
# ZAP Lesson 1: Overview and Modes

## Overview
OWASP ZAP is an intercepting proxy for testing web applications. It sits between your browser and the target so you can observe and analyze traffic as you explore the application.

## How ZAP works
- Runs locally as a proxy
- Records and analyzes requests and responses
- Raises alerts via passive and active scanners

## ZAP modes
- Safe: no potentially dangerous actions
- Protected: only allows potentially dangerous actions on in-scope URLs
- Standard: does not restrict actions
- Attack: actively scans new in-scope nodes as they are discovered

## Recommended baseline
Start in Protected mode and define scope before scanning. This reduces the chance of scanning the wrong target.

## Quick Start
The Quick Start tab provides a streamlined workflow for scanning a target you are authorized to test.
examples: Explain why Protected mode prevents accidental out-of-scope scans.
Identify when Attack mode is appropriate in a controlled test environment.
Describe how Quick Start fits into a basic test workflow.
objectives: Describe ZAP as an intercepting proxy.
